channel.title: NYT > Most Recent Headlines
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: U.S. Average Gas Price Returns to $4 a Gallon as Iran Crisis Escalates
item.description: A month ago, drivers were enjoying some relief at the pump after the U.S. and Iran signed a deal intended to reopen the Strait of Hormuz. Now, prices are rising again.
item.dc:creator: Katie Robertson
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Gabriel V. Cárdenas for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Gasoline prices, which had been falling in recent weeks, are almost back up to where they were a month ago.

item.title: Mi hijo murió a manos de ICE. Exijo que haya rendición de cuentas
item.description: Ningún estadounidense que haya vivido una tragedia como la mía debería tener que luchar para obtener respuestas de su propio gobierno.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Reyes

media:content.media:credit: Harmon Li para The New York Times

item.title: Micah Lasher, Congressman in Waiting, Has a Few Tricks Up His Sleeve
item.description: Mr. Lasher, the likely successor to Representative Jerry Nadler, has an unusual back story: As a teenager, he was a prodigy who performed magic tricks on national television.
item.dc:creator: Emma Goldberg

media:content.media:description: Assemblyman Micah Lasher’s proficiency as a child magician led to a book published by Simon and Schuster, “The Magic of Micah Lasher.”

item.title: Building Community with $1 Coffees, a Salsa Club and Patience
item.description: The 14th Street Y in the East Village has worked to become a social gathering place in a city where sharing space does not always mean building a connection.
item.dc:creator: Winnie Hu
item.category: Jewish Community Relations Council of New York

media:content.media:description: Leonardo Sardella, a dance instructor, emphasizes fun over learning steps in his classes at the 14th Street Y’s salsa club.

item.title: NYT Spelling Bee Answers for July 20, 2026
item.description: Feeling stuck on today’s puzzle? We can help.
item.dc:creator: New York Times Games

item.title: Spain Wins 2026 World Cup
item.description: Fans of Spain’s men’s national soccer team celebrated its World Cup victory on Sunday after Spain beat Argentina, 1-0, in extra time.
item.dc:creator: Cynthia Silva and Shawn Paik

item.title: Rubio Says That U.S. Is Still Open to Diplomacy With Iran
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio also said that Iran has signaled it wants to negotiate with the United States, even as the two sides escalated their attacks.
item.dc:creator: Yan Zhuang
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:credit: Brendan Smialowski/Agence France-Presse, Pool/AFP via Getty Images
media:content.media:description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio speaking to reporters at Joint Base Andrews in Maryland, on Sunday night.

item.title: Spain Rejoices and Argentina Weeps as 2026 World Cup Ends
item.description: See how crowds in Madrid and in Rosario, Argentina, took in the tournament’s nail-biter final. Spain prevailed, 1-0.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times

media:content.media:credit: Joseph Fox for The New York Times

item.title: ‘Ransom Canyon,’ and 5 More Shows to Watch on TV This Week
item.description: The second season of the cowboy drama airs. And a documentary shows the behind the scenes of the “Heartstopper: Forever” movie.
item.dc:creator: Shivani Gonzalez

media:content.media:description: From left: Josh Duhamel and Jack Schumacher in “Ransom Canyon.”

item.title: World Cup 2026: See How Spain Finally Broke Through Argentina’s Defense in Extra Time
item.description: View in 3-D the single goal that secured Spain’s World Cup title against Argentina, the reigning champion.
item.dc:creator: Bora Erden, Malika Khurana, Allison McCann, Karthik Patanjali and Raj Saha

item.title: India’s CJP Party Leads Protest March to Parliament After Exam Leaks
item.description: The demonstrations were prompted in part after questions for the country’s biggest medical school entrance exam were leaked, forcing millions to retake the test.
item.dc:creator: Mujib Mashal and Pragati K.B.

media:content.media:description: Police confronting people gathered ahead of a planned march by supporters of India’s Cockroach Janta Party toward Parliament in New Delhi on the opening day of its monsoon session on Monday.

item.title: The Things Left Behind in the Hong Kong Apartment Fire
item.description: Months after a deadly fire in Hong Kong killed their loved ones, they went home to survey the ruins. Here’s what they brought back with them.
item.dc:creator: Anjali Tsui and Lam Yik Fei

item.title: Tehran and Washington Face a ‘Wake Up Moment’ Over the War
item.description: U.S. and Iranian attacks are unfolding in a way that risks a wider war they cannot contain, analysts warn, unless they can return to negotiations quickly.
item.dc:creator: Erika Solomon
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi/Polaris for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Iranians, like these people walking under a poster of the country’s three supreme leaders, are growing increasingly frustrated with the war.

item.title: Five of the Main Challenges Awaiting Andy Burnham When He Becomes Prime Minister
item.description: Andy Burnham, who is set to enter Downing Street on Monday, faces a long and daunting list of issues. Here are some of the most urgent.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Castle

media:content.media:description: The Labour Party’s Andy Burnham during an event in June in Manchester, England.

item.title: No Corrections: July 20, 2026
item.description: No corrections appeared in print on Monday, July 20, 2026.

item.title: Quote of the Day: Heartbreak of Quakes Is Doubled for Venezuela’s Many Migrants
item.description: Quotation of the Day for Monday, July 20, 2026.

item.title: Trump Says New Air Force One Will Be Taken Out of Service to Be ‘Maxed Out’
item.description: The Qatari-donated plane has been under scrutiny over whether it is fitted with sufficient defensive capabilities to adequately protect a U.S. president and others traveling on it.
item.dc:creator: Maggie Haberman

media:content.media:description: President Trump exiting the Qatari-donated jet earlier this month in South Dakota.

item.title: Family of Five Found Dead After Storms Flooded Utah Canyon
item.description: A Provo fire captain, his wife and their three sons were found dead over the weekend near a campground in southern Utah. They were likely flood victims, the authorities said.
item.dc:creator: Sejal Govindarao

media:content.media:description: Provo Fire and Rescue Captain Spencer Long was found dead along with four members of his family near Sunglow Campground in Utah.

item.title: ‘House of the Dragon’ Season 3, Episode 5 Recap: Under Pressure
item.description: Queen Rhaenrya is beginning to sound a little paranoid. That rarely bodes well with an autocrat.
item.dc:creator: Sean T. Collins

media:content.media:description: Rhaenyra (Emma D’Arcy, right) is right when she tells Daemon (Matt Smith) there is a mastermind behind the violence in the city. What’s worrisome is how she talks about it.

item.title: Spain Fans Celebrate World Cup Victory
item.description: Fans of Spain’s men’s national soccer team celebrated its World Cup victory on Sunday after Spain beat Argentina, 1-0, in extra time.
item.dc:creator: Cynthia Silva

media:content.media:credit: John Todd/ISI Photos/ISI Photos, via Getty Images

item.title: Tears in Argentina for Lionel Messi’s ‘Last Tango’
item.description: There were still fireworks and smoke bombs, but the nation was disappointed for its soccer star after he lost in his likely last World Cup.
item.dc:creator: Emma Bubola

media:content.media:credit: Sebastián López Brach for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: A watch party in Rosario, Argentina, Lionel Messi’s hometown, turned somber.

item.title: Did This World Cup Change Soccer Forever?
item.description: With a wartime host nation, a referee deported and a ban overturned after a call from the White House, the 2026 tournament tested FIFA like no other.
item.dc:creator: Tariq Panja

media:content.media:credit: Joseph Fox for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Fans watching the World Cup final in Madrid on Sunday. Spain won 1-0.

item.title: JD and Usha Vance Welcome Fourth Child
item.description: In a post on social media, Mr. Vance said his wife and their son, Alec Neel Vance, were “happy and healthy.”
item.dc:creator: Alexandra E. Petri

media:content.media:credit: Anna Rose Layden for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Vice President JD Vance and Usha Vance during an April event at the White House.

item.title: Watching the World Cup: ‘I Can’t Believe We Get to Be Here’
item.description: For fans at MetLife Stadium, the World Cup final was the thrill of a lifetime.
item.dc:creator: Christopher Maag

media:content.media:credit: Vincent Alban for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Members of the Spanish team celebrate their victory over Argentina on Sunday.

item.title: Global Oil Price Crosses $90 a Barrel as U.S.-Iran Conflict Widens
item.description: Brent crude, the global benchmark for oil, rose more than 3 percent as trading resumed on Sunday, while S&P 500 futures remained essentially unchanged.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca F. Elliott
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: Hal Williams, Actor in First Wave of Black Sitcoms, Dies at 91
item.description: As a divorced father of three in Ohio, he moved to Hollywood to pursue his boyhood dream to act. He became a recognizable face on “Sanford and Son.”
item.dc:creator: Alex Traub

media:content.media:description: Hal Williams, second from left, as Officer “Smitty” Smith on “Sanford and Son,” the role for which he was best known. With him, from left, are Howard Platt, Redd Foxx and, in the background, Demond Wilson.

item.title: NYT Crossword Answers for July 20, 2026
item.description: Eric Rollfing senses an opportunity.
item.dc:creator: Sam Corbin

media:content.media:credit: Dan Koeck for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Gallop, don’t trot, to solve this light and breezy Monday crossword.

item.title: First World Cup Halftime Show: Lots of Stars, Little Purpose
item.description: The performers valiantly attempted to justify the existence of the show, which was impeccably choreographed and thematically jumbled.
item.dc:creator: Lindsay Zoladz

media:content.media:description: The first World Cup halftime show had a simple message of love and unity.

item.title: Trump Relents From Spain-Bashing to Present Its Team the World Cup Trophy
item.description: Mr. Trump, who this month referred to Spain as “hopeless,” seemingly had little choice but to exhibit diplomacy on Sunday as he handed the golden trophy to the country’s players.
item.dc:creator: Neil Vigdor and Christopher Maag
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:description: President Trump presents Lamine Yamal with a medal after Spain’s victory over Argentina at the 2026 World Cup final in New Jersey on Sunday.

item.title: A Conversation with Zohran Mamdani
item.description: The mayor of New York City talks about Andy Burnham, Netanyahu and the controversial U.S. red card.
item.dc:creator: Katrin Bennhold
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Vincent Alban for The New York Times

item.title: Deadly 5.5-Magnitude Earthquake Strikes Peru
item.description: A 5.5-magnitude earthquake struck Peru’s central highlands region on Saturday, killing six people, the authorities said. Although Peru is prone to frequent quakes, this was the biggest to strike that region in over 50 years.
item.dc:creator: Jorge Mitssunaga

media:content.media:description: Una casa en Chupuro, Perú, que fue destruida por el terremoto el sábado.

item.title: Madonna, BTS and More Bring Spectacle to World Cup Halftime
item.description: Shakira and Justin Bieber were among the other performers in a star-packed performance that aims to mimic the Super Bowl halftime show’s cultural success.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times

item.title: Terremoto en Perú deja 6 muertos y desplaza a cientos
item.description: Perú es propenso a sismos frecuentes, pero el temblor de magnitud 5,5 fue el más grande en azotar la zona de la sierra central en casi 50 años.
item.dc:creator: Mitra Taj

media:content.media:description: Una casa en Chupuro, Perú, que fue destruida por el terremoto el sábado.

item.title: Russia Bombards Kyiv in One of the War’s Largest Missile Assaults
item.description: Officials said one person had been killed and more than 15 had been injured, with that toll likely to rise. The attacks on Kyiv coincided with Ukraine’s targeting of oil and gas facilities in Russia, which has prompted fuel shortages there.
item.dc:creator: McKinnon de Kuyper

media:content.media:description: A helicopter dropping water on Sunday over the site of a Russian missile strike in the Kyiv region of Ukraine.

item.title: Two More Patients Die in Outbreak of Legionnaires’ Disease in Manhattan
item.description: The deaths brought the toll in the outbreak to three. The second person who died was an 88-year-old retired businessman who lived on the Upper East Side, his lawyer said.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Haag and Joseph Goldstein

media:content.media:credit: Mimi d’Autremont for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The city’s health department ordered 76 buildings to clean and disinfect their cooling systems as part of its operation to address an outbreak of Legionnaires’ disease.

item.title: Earthquake in Peru Kills 6 and Displaces Hundreds
item.description: Peru is prone to frequent quakes, but the 5.5-magnitude temblor was the biggest to strike the central highlands area in more than 50 years.
item.dc:creator: Mitra Taj

media:content.media:description: Una casa en Chupuro, Perú, que fue destruida por el terremoto el sábado.

item.title: In Arizona’s Primaries, Democrats Fight Over a House Seat They Need Badly
item.description: Democrats have a chance to flip a competitive battleground district in and around Scottsdale, but the winner of Tuesday’s primary will emerge bloodied and broke.
item.dc:creator: Sejal Govindarao

media:content.media:credit: Cassidy Araiza for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Gerrymandering has drastically narrowed the playing field for midterm elections.

item.title: Nolan’s ‘The Odyssey’ Brings in $124.5 Million at the Box Office on Opening Weekend
item.description: Christopher Nolan’s nearly three-hour epic was No. 1 over the weekend, with about $124.5 million in ticket sales in the United States and Canada.
item.dc:creator: Brooks Barnes

item.title: Tropical Storm Watches Issued Along the Gulf Coast
item.description: A tropical depression could soon strengthen into the hurricane season’s second named storm, forecasters said.
item.dc:creator: Nazaneen Ghaffar

item.title: The U.S. Is Sending More Warplanes to the Middle East, Officials Say
item.description: F-16 and F-35 jets were en route to the region from Europe, according to U.S. officials who were not authorized to speak publicly.
item.dc:creator: Eric Schmitt
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:description: Fighter jets stationed in Germany and Britain are being deployed to the Middle East according to officials.

item.title: Man Killed by Truck After ICE Encounter in Florida Was Visiting From Mexico
item.description: A Central Florida nonprofit group identified the man as Juan Jairo Coronilla Durán and said he had planned to return home to his family.
item.dc:creator: David Ovalle

media:content.media:description: A man lays a bouquet of flowers during a vigil for Juan Jairo Coronilla Durán in St. Augustine, Fla.

item.title: Iran War Live Updates: Another U.S. Service Member Is Killed as War Expands
item.description: The Pentagon said a service member was killed in northern Iraq while troops disposed of an Iranian drone. It also said “unidentified remains” were found in Jordan, where an earlier attack had left two soldiers dead and another missing.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times

media:content.media:description: A satellite image of the Muwaffaq Salti Air Base, in Azraq, Jordan, which came under Iranian fire last week.

item.title: World Cup Updates: Cheers in Spain and Tears in Argentina After Nail-Biter Final
item.dc:creator: The New York Times

media:content.media:credit: Vincent Alban for The New York Times
